criterion: CAL-01 through CAL-05 and BT-01 in acceptance_results.csv
condition: OOT O/E=1.128, intercept=0.345, slope=1.096, ECE=0.013, Brier=0.0690 vs null=0.0826, and quarterly interval coverage=100%.
impact: Absolute default risk may be misstated under shifted conditions even when rank ordering remains useful.
recommendation: Recalibrate on recent representative data and confirm performance on a later held-out period.

impact: Changing population or economic mix can weaken calibration and segment reliability.

condition: Challenger-minus-champion OOT AUC=-0.052 (paired 95% CI -0.067 to -0.036); relative Brier improvement=-9.1%.
impact: A benchmark can reveal nonlinear performance opportunity but introduces explainability trade-offs.
recommendation: Assess redevelopment if material gains persist; otherwise retain the transparent champion and repeat benchmarking at redevelopment.
closure_evidence: Documented benchmark comparison on a later OOT sample using the same population, features, and metrics.
